title: Tiny Multi-Task Sequence Sidecar as OOF Meta-Features
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop papers report that Bi-LSTM-style models and residual multi-task designs exploit lap-to-lap temporal structure that tabular boosters may compress away.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop papers report that Bi-LSTM-style models and residual multi-task designs exploit lap-to-lap temporal structure that tabular boosters may compress away. Since the current GBDT baseline is already strong, the safer play is a small sequential sidecar, not a full replacement.
implementation_hint: For each Year-Race-Driver sequence, feed the last K laps of raw and derived numeric features into a small Bi-LSTM/TCN or residual MLP. Predict both PitNextLap and laps_to_next_pit or will_pit_before_finish. Train only on candidate rows near the pit window, generate strict OOF predictions, and add those predictions as meta-features to the main tabular model.
expected_effect: Captures non-linear acceleration toward a stop, reset dynamics after pits, and short temporal motifs that hand-made lag features miss; likely a small but additive gain.
risk: High overfit risk under weak CV and low prevalence; keep the model tiny, regularized, and OOF-only.
